steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank must fit between “her” and “*Gothic*” in the phrase “her ______ *Gothic* (2020)”. The noun “novel” needs to connect properly to “Mexican Gothic,” which is a specific title naming that novel.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
When a title directly identifies or renames a noun, it functions as an appositive and requires a comma to separate the general term from the specific name. “Her novel, Mexican Gothic” correctly uses a comma before the title that identifies the work. Option A lacks punctuation (creating an awkward ungrammatical sequence), Option B uses a colon (which introduces definitions or lists, not appositives), and Option D is incomplete.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
